title: What we know about screen time and social media in early adolescence: a review of findings from the Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development Study.

FT_IC1.evidence: Review of ABCD Study data with over 11,000 participants; explicitly cites ABCD Study as source

FT_IC2.evidence: Reviews associations with specific smartphone/social media variables: social media use, video games, video chat, videos, texting

FT_IC3.evidence: Review article that summarizes findings from other ABCD studies; does not present original inferential statistical tests conducted by the authors

FT_IC4.evidence: Review article; no numeric coefficients or statistics reported as original analysis

FT_IC5.evidence: This is a review article (title: 'a review of findings from the ABCD Study'; journal: Current Opinion in Pediatrics, a review venue). Review articles are explicitly excluded per FT-IC5 requirement for 'original empirical ABCD analysis'

FT_IC6.evidence: Published journal article (Current Opinion in Pediatrics, 2025, DOI: 10.1097/mop.0000000000001462)

FT_EC7_reason: Not applicable; FT-IC5 failed. Paper is a review, not original empirical analysis, and is excluded on this basis before FT-EC7 evaluation.
notes: C1 vs C2 disagreement resolved: C2 correctly identified this as a review article (NOT_MET on FT-IC5). C1 marked FT-IC5 as UNKNOWN due to lack of PDF, but the title and journal clearly indicate this is a review. Review articles fail the FT-IC5 requirement for original empirical analysis.
